title: 01 campus buildings migrate
prompt: REFERENCE AUTHORITY — Images 1 and 2 lock Liz and John, their wardrobe, Peelerz bags, campus-step entrance, and warm live-action register. They do not lock the later world. ONE RULE — At Liz and John's shared first bite, every campus building inhales, lifts its complete foundation on four enormous architectural legs, and begins a quiet seasonal migration across the horizon. The original stone step beneath the couple is the only architecture that stays grounded. FRAME — Deadpan daylight super-wide from slightly below the steps. Liz and John remain normal-size at center, smiling in recognition with their Peelerz bags. Behind them, a library, gymnasium, dormitory, clock tower, and lecture hall stride in one majestic herd at different distances; each carries attached lawns, trees, sidewalks, and tiny calmly commuting students. One moving lecture hall lowers a staircase so students can board while it walks. Buildings have no faces and are not monsters. Their legs remain made from their own columns, foundations, and staircases. VISUAL PROOF — fixed couple and step; five recognizable moving buildings; complete land parcels traveling with them; ordinary people treating migration as the new commute. AVOID — earthquake, destruction, floating buildings, giant animals disguised as buildings, fantasy castle, crowd panic.

resolved_params.system_prompt: Create one extraordinary but coherent cinematic Peelerz commercial styleframe, never a collage, contact sheet, storyboard grid, or design sheet. Ben's brief is an open canvas: favor a bold whole- world creative situation over improving any existing car-peel, parachute, or mango-mecha idea. Preserve only the explicitly declared talent/location authorities. Make the prompt's single impossible rule visible at world scale with one dominant verb, clear spatial organization, and a youthful sense of wonder. The result can be excessive, mythic, psychedelic, deadpan, or impossible, but must remain benign, appetizing, and not preschool-coded. No car opening into petals, no parachute, no robot/mecha armor, no construction-helper crew, no rainbow arch, no generic cheering gummy crowd, no captions, labels, borders, watermarks, gore, weapons, or invented package copy.
